user_query:
I’ve saved snapshots of brewery search results from several cities. Please compile a single ranked shortlist using only these files:
- input/portland_breweries.txt
- input/seattle_breweries.txt
- input/bend_breweries.txt

Each file contains lines in this format (two lines per brewery):
"🍺 <Name> — <City>, <State>, <Type>" on one line, followed by the website URL on the next line.

Task:
1) Merge all breweries from the three files.
2) For each brewery, compute a score using this rule:
   - Type weights: micro=3, brewpub=2, regional=1, all other types (large, planning, bar, contract, proprietor, closed, or anything else)=0.
   - Website bonus: +1 if a non-empty website URL is present.
3) Rank breweries by total score (desc). Break ties by brewery name A→Z (case-insensitive).
4) Normalize the type in outputs to the canonical lowercase label from this set: {micro, brewpub, regional, large, planning, bar, contract, proprietor, closed}. (If a type isn’t one of these, set it to "closed" only if the snapshot says Closed, otherwise treat it as 0-weight and map it to its closest canonical form if obvious; if not obvious, leave it as-is but lowercase.)
5) Write two files:
   - output/shortlist.json: an array of exactly the top 5 breweries with objects of the form {"name", "city", "state", "type", "website", "score"} in the exact ranked order.
   - output/summary.md: first, a 5-item bullet list in the same order as the shortlist with lines formatted exactly as: "- <Name> — <City>, <State> (<type>) — <website>". Then a blank line, then a header line "Counts by state:", then one line per state in these inputs with exact counts of breweries aggregated across all three files, formatted exactly as: "Oregon: <N>" and "Washington: <M>" (no other states).

Notes:
- Do not call any external services; work strictly from the provided snapshots under input/.
- Treat the long dash (—) in the input literally when parsing.
- Ensure output paths are exactly output/shortlist.json and output/summary.md.
